introduction--docs.name: Docs
introduction--docs.title: Introduction
introduction--docs.content:
import LinkTo from "@storybook/addon-links/react";
import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

{/* prettier-ignore */}
<Meta title="Introduction" />

# React Design System

My custom design system on top of shadcn/ui + Base UI, with a bunch of components to be able to fast prototype new pages and products

## ⚠️ Important Notice

**This is a personal design system** built for my own projects and experimentation.

- **Breaking changes are expected** and may occur at any time without notice
- **Not recommended for production use** in external projects
- **Best used as a reference**: Study the patterns, component structure, and testing approaches to build your own design system
- API stability is not guaranteed between versions

If you choose to use this library, I strongly recommend forking it or using it as inspiration for your own custom design system rather than depending on it directly.

## Start Now

- [Installation](?path=/docs/installation--docs)
- [Usage](?path=/docs/usage--docs)

## Components

- [Components](?path=/docs/components--docs)
- [Layouts](?path=/docs/layouts-layouts--docs)

examples-ai-composition-patterns--docs.title: Examples/AI Composition Patterns
examples-ai-composition-patterns--docs.content:
import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; # AI Composition Patterns ## Dashboard shell ```tsx import { DrawerLayout, Header, RemoteDataTable, ValueCard, ValueItem, ValueText, } from "@pautena/react-design-system";
; ``` ## Form flow with dialogs ```tsx import { FormDialog, ConfirmDialog, TextField } from "@pautena/react-design-system"; ``` ## Data exploration layout ```tsx import { ListPanel, RemoteDataTable, ExpandableAlert } from "@pautena/react-design-system"; setOpen(false)} /> ; ``` ## Navigation + tabs ```tsx import { TabCard, TabCardPanel } from "@pautena/react-design-system"; Summary content Detailed content ; ``` ## AI prompt examples - "Create a dashboard with DrawerLayout, a header, 3 metric cards, and a RemoteDataTable." - "Build a form wizard using TabCard, TextField, Select, and ConfirmDialog." - "Show a data explorer with ListPanel navigation and an ExpandableAlert tip section."

examples-ai-mcp-usage--docs.name: Docs
examples-ai-mcp-usage--docs.title: Examples/AI MCP Usage
examples-ai-mcp-usage--docs.content:
import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Examples/AI MCP Usage" />

# AI MCP Usage

The design system publishes a registry for MCP-enabled tools.

## Discover components

- Registry manifest: `https://react-design-system.pautena.com/registry/index.json`
- Component JSON: `https://react-design-system.pautena.com/registry/value-card.json`

## Example AI workflow

1. Search the registry for `remote-data-table`.
2. Install the component via your MCP client.
3. Generate code using the exported types and usage metadata.

## Example prompts

```
"Show available components for data display in @pautena registry"
"Install @pautena/remote-data-table and add it to a dashboard"
"Generate a form wizard with TextField, Select, Autocomplete, and ConfirmDialog"
```

## Recommended prompts for code generation

```
Create a dashboard using @pautena components with:
- DrawerLayout navigation
- Header with actions
- ValueCard metrics
- RemoteDataTable with query filters
```

## MCP integration checklist

- Registry JSON available in `public/r/`
- Storybook addon-mcp configured
- Components annotated with registry metadata

components--docs.title: Components
components--docs.content:
import { Canvas, Description, Meta, Story } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

{/* prettier-ignore */}
<Meta title="Components" />

# Components

Browse components by category:

## [Forms](?path=/docs/forms--docs)
Form controls and field inputs - text field, autocomplete, and select.

## [Inputs](?path=/docs/inputs--docs)
Input-related components - date range calendar and date range picker.

## [Data Display](?path=/docs/data-display--docs)
Present information - labels, headers, boards, value components, cards.

## [Dialogs](?path=/docs/dialogs--docs)
Modal overlays - confirmation dialogs, form dialogs, bootstrap dialogs.

## [Containers](?path=/docs/containers--docs)
Content wrappers and organizational components.

## [Placeholders](?path=/docs/placeholders--docs)
Loading states and empty content indicators.

## [Tables](?path=/docs/tables--docs)
Data grids and table components for structured data display.

## [Alerts](?path=/docs/alerts--docs)
User notifications and alert messages.

## [Feedback](?path=/docs/feedback--docs)
User feedback components - loading indicators, notifications.

## [Navigation](?path=/docs/navigation--docs)
Navigation components - drawers, menus, tabs.

---

**Tip**: Use the sidebar search to quickly find specific components.

installation--docs.name: Docs
installation--docs.title: Installation
installation--docs.content:
import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

{/* prettier-ignore */}
<Meta title="Installation" />

# Installation

This project is available as an npm package [@pautena/react-design-system](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@pautena/react-design-system).

## Prerequisites

- **Node.js**: v18+ recommended
- **React**: v17+, v18+, or v19+

## Install Package

Using npm:

```bash
npm install @pautena/react-design-system
```

Using yarn:

```bash
yarn add @pautena/react-design-system
```

## Styles

Import the library stylesheet once in your app entrypoint:

```jsx
import "@pautena/react-design-system/global.css";
```

layouts--docs.content:
import { Description, Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

{/* prettier-ignore */}
<Meta title="Layouts" />

# Layouts

The layouts are components used to define the structure and organization of the user interface.
They are reusable components that help in creating consistent layouts across different pages or sections of the application.
These components typically handle the arrangement of headers, footers, sidebars, content areas, and other common UI elements.

## DrawerLayout

import { DrawerLayout } from "@/components/layouts/drawer-layout/drawer-layout";

<Description of={DrawerLayout} />

[Drawer Layout Stories](?path=/docs/layouts-drawerlayout--docs)

## HeaderLayout

import { HeaderLayout } from "@/components/layouts/header-layout/header-layout";

<Description of={HeaderLayout} />

[Header Layout Stories](?path=/docs/layouts-headerlayout--docs)

shadcn-introduction--docs.name: Docs
shadcn-introduction--docs.title: shadcn/Introduction
shadcn-introduction--docs.content:
# shadcn/ui Components This library currently keeps only the base `Button` and `Badge` in `src/components/ui/`. ## Available Components - **Button** - Action button with variants and sizes. - **Badge** - Compact status/tag element with variants. ## Interactive Showcase Visit the **shadcn/Button** and **shadcn/Badge** stories to see the currently available components. ## Examples See the **shadcn/Button** and **shadcn/Badge** stories for variants. ## Theme System All components use CSS variables defined in `src/styles/globals.css`: ```css :root { --background: 0 0% 100%; --foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%; --primary: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%; --secondary: 210 40% 96.1%; /* ... more variables */ } .dark { --background: 222.2 84% 4.9%; --foreground: 210 40% 98%; /* ... dark mode values */ } ``` ## Usage Import components from `@/components/ui/`: ```tsx import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"; function MyComponent() { return (
New
); } ```
